summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a brief meeting to consider text amendments related to storage/shipping containers and accessory structure definitions (Zoning Case 2024-005). The amendments, initiated by the trustees and already recommended for approval by Clermont County, clarify the permitted and accessory use tables, add shipping containers as a permitted accessory use with specific standards, and clean up redundant definitions. The commission unanimously recommended approval of all proposed text amendments to the Board of Trustees.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from April 2, 2024
Motion to forgo chairman's statement passed (no audience or persons to be sworn in)
Motion to recommend approval of Zoning Case 2024-005 text amendments related to storage containers and accessory structures passed unanimously
action_items: Staff to forward Zoning Commission recommendation of approval for Zoning Case 2024-005 to the Board of Trustees
Staff to confirm whether a Zoning Commission meeting will be held on July 2nd (to be determined by next day)
key_amounts: 1 shipping container allowed on lots 1 acre or smaller
2 shipping containers allowed on lots between 1 and 5 acres
3 shipping containers allowed on lots 5 acres or greater
notable_topics: Text amendments to Goshen Township Zoning Resolution regarding shipping containers as accessory structures
Clarification that unlisted uses in permitted and accessory use tables are prohibited
Shipping container standards: hard surface foundation, solid color paint consistent with principal structure, no advertisements or signage
Cleanup of redundant definitions for accessory buildings, structures, and uses in Section 16.03
Addition of shipping container definition to zoning resolution
attendees: Brandon (staff or member who seconded motion)
notes: Meeting lasted approximately 12 minutes, noted as possibly a record short meeting. No public audience was present. The text amendments originated from a recent BZA case where someone requested shipping containers as accessory structures, and the standards were developed based on BZA recommendations and trustee discussions. Clermont County had already recommended approval of the amendments.
title: ZC 6-4-24
